task_updates.executor_notes: Added capacity database helpers in `gpu_orchestrator/database.py`: intent insert and outcome update, latest authoritative intent read, shadow observation reads, TTL pool lease acquire/release, route backoff read/failure/reset with `__pool_floor__` normalization and reset-on-success semantics, direct root-level `system_logs` metadata insert, optional capacity metadata on `create_worker_record`, and worker lookup by `capacity_intent_id` plus `capacity_action_ordinal`. Existing `create_worker_record(worker_id, instance_type, runpod_id=None)` callers remain compatible because all capacity fields are keyword-only optional additions. Added `tests/gpu_orchestrator/test_database_capacity_helpers.py` with a fake Supabase client covering each helper surface. Verified with `python -m pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/test_database_capacity_helpers.py tests/scripts/test_sql_migrations.py -q` and `python -m py_compile gpu_orchestrator/database.py`. Full suite was run with `python -m pytest --tb=no -q --no-header`; it failed only at the already observed `tests/test_module_import_coverage.py::test_runpod_cutover_modules_import_at_runtime` because `scripts.spot_instance_lifetime_test` is missing, unrelated to T2.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The new `DatabaseClient` helpers cover intent pre-act insert and outcome update, authoritative and shadow intent reads, normalized route backoff read/update/reset, TTL pool lease acquire/release, direct `system_logs` insertion with root-level metadata, capacity metadata on worker creation, and idempotency lookup by capacity intent/action. Focused fake-Supabase tests exercise those surfaces without requiring live database state.
